item.title: A Brutal First for the Cruise Industry
item.description: A hantavirus outbreak is serious and unnerving.
item.dc:creator: Katherine J. Wu

item.title: Iran’s Unexpected Resilience
item.description: Two months of fighting have emphasized some of the country’s advantages.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: Yet Another Wasted Met Gala
item.description: The evening squandered an opportunity to really make the case for fashion as art.
item.dc:creator: Shirley Li

item.title: Progressive Activists Are Sometimes on the Wrong Side of History
item.description: Thinking otherwise can enable the left’s worst instincts, as a speech at the University of Michigan’s commencement showed.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Judicial Supremacy Has Arrived
item.description: Last week’s Supreme Court decision didn’t just undermine the Voting Rights Act. It foreclosed the possibility of any new Voting Rights Act in the future, too.
item.dc:creator: Duncan Hosie

item.title: Photos: The Rescue of Timmy the Whale
item.description: Efforts to rescue a humpback whale off the coast of Germany led to a successful release after it had been stranded for most of the past month. The whale, named Timmy by local media, was eventually pulled into a barge and towed to the North Sea.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: What Adding Race to BMI Can Do
item.description: Race-sensitive cutoffs can address BMI’s shortcomings, but not entirely.
item.dc:creator: Katherine J. Wu

item.title: The Secret of Elizabeth Strout’s Appeal
item.description: How she writes best sellers that are also critical darlings
item.dc:creator: Adam Begley

item.title: The House of Representatives Is Turning Into the Electoral College
item.description: Thanks to the Supreme Court, the gerrymandering wars, already awful, are poised to get even worse.
item.dc:creator: Marc Novicoff

item.title: For Ibram X. Kendi, It’s Nazis All the Way Down
item.description: His new book describes the “Great Replacement” theory as a convoluted plot, but fails to explain why it appeals to people in the first place.
item.dc:creator: Gal Beckerman

item.title: Democrats Could Use a Cold Shower Before the Midterms
item.description: They have good reason to be optimistic. But they are sounding a bit too giddy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Leibovich

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Lhotse Facelift
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: My Role as a ‘Complicit’ Journalist
item.description: Algorithms turn nuanced articles into rage bait that helps fuel political violence.
item.dc:creator: Michael Scherer

item.title: How the Supreme Court Came to Accept a Practice It Called Unjust
item.description: The Court went from condemning partisan gerrymandering to effectively encouraging it.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Europe Without America
item.description: The Iran war has given European leaders new impetus to plan for self-defense.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: The Candidate From ICE
item.description: A GOP primary in Ohio will test Trump’s mass-deportation push.
item.dc:creator: Nick Miroff

item.title: Why Stocks Keep Going Up
item.description: The boom is not as untethered from reality as it may look.
item.dc:creator: James Surowiecki

item.title: The End of Cigarettes Is Coming
item.description: The U.K. is phasing out smoking. How long will Americans tolerate tobacco—and other vices?
item.dc:creator: Charles Fain Lehman

item.title: More Photos From Artemis II
item.description: NASA just released more than 12,000 images from their recent 10-day trip around the moon and back, collecting photographs taken by the four astronauts aboard and automated systems on the Orion spacecraft.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Why One Coach’s Personal Life Is a Sports-Wide Scandal
item.description: Mike Vrabel is facing questions about his personal relationship with a reporter. He should answer them.
item.dc:creator: Jemele Hill
